rationale: Both correctly diagnose the issue as a run-level misconfiguration (default core_model → dummy weights) matching the thread's 'invalid' resolution, cite the same PR #4354 and comparable code paths, and give the correct --run-level=full_model fix. X is more complete: it explains the specific bizarre log symptoms (repeated 'Joe' collapse, noisy audio) as signatures of an uninitialized model, flags the video-path and hardware prerequisites for a real pass, and adds a useful forward-looking ergonomics suggestion. X's grounding is broader but includes some suspiciously precise/unverifiable citations (e.g. a pytest marker claim and four scattered assertions.py line numbers) not corroborated by the thread, giving it a slight embellishment risk, while Y's narrower citation set is more conservative but also thinner on completeness.
